figures.caption: Figure 1: Comparison of representation structures. Factorization constrains the joint score landscape to the intersection of independent state and action profiles.

Figure 2: Learning curves across 8 DM Control Suite tasks spanning four morphologies. FaStR’s gains are largest on tasks whose dynamics admit a CP factorization, consistent with the bias-estimation view. Shaded: ±1 std across 5 seeds.

Table 2: Hyperparameters for FaStR and the five baselines used in Table 1. Values shared across all methods (discount γ=0.99, 1×106 environment frames, replay capacity 1×106, action repeat 2, 5 seeds, 10 evaluation episodes every 10,000 frames) are described in Section B.1.

Figure 5: Empirical CP misfit diagnostic across 13 DM Control Suite tasks. Each marker is one task; the x-axis is the test-set excess loss of the CP predictor over the joint predictor (positive means CP is worse), measured by (a) NCE misfit ∆̂NCE
